primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivable for formal hedge accounting of foreign exchange risk at consolidated level

primary_authority.reason: IFRS 9 defines hedged items and provides the exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: IFRIC 16 clarifies the nature of the hedged risk for intragroup items and that hedging instruments may be held by any entity within the group.
IFRS 9 specifies the default accounting for foreign exchange differences on financial assets, recognised in profit or loss unless part of a hedging relationship.
IFRS 9 sets criteria for hedged items such as reliable measurability and, for forecast transactions, high probability.

peripheral_authority.reason: IFRIC 17 addresses accounting for dividends to owners, but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility.
IFRIC 2 deals with members' shares and redemption rights, unrelated to intragroup dividend hedge accounting.
SIC-25 addresses changes in tax status, not relevant to foreign exchange hedge accounting.
IAS 32 deals with classification of financial instruments as liabilities or equity, not directly addressing hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivable.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting eligibility for foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivable
Default accounting for foreign exchange risk without hedge designation

approaches.label: Foreign exchange hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16, foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable may be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements, provided the hedge accounting criteria are met.

approaches.label: Non-hedge foreign exchange accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If no hedge designation is made, foreign exchange differences on the intragroup dividend receivable are recognised in profit or loss in accordance with IFRS 9 default treatment.
